sections_cited: section 47 of the Sales Tax Act

chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Special STRA 255 of 2018 ___________________________________________________________ DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) ___________________________________________________________ 1. For orders on CMA No.2156/2018. 2. For orders on office objection/reply. 3. For orders on CMA No.2157/2018. 4. For hearing of main case. 06.04.2026 Matter is pending since 2018 without any progress. On previous four dates following order was passed: “17.08.2018 Ms. Fazila Amber, advocate for the applicant. 1. Granted. 2. Learned counsel for the applicant requests for time to comply with the office objection. One week’s time is granted to comply with the office objections. To come up on 05.09.2018 as requested by the learned counsel for the applicant.” “05.9.2018 Mr. Mansoor Ali, advocate for applicant. Learned counsel for the applicant seeks time to prepare the case. Adjourned.” “28.11.2018 Mr. S. Maqbool Hussain, advocate undertakes to file Vakalatnama on behalf of applicant and requests for time to prepare the case. Time is grated. Adjourned” “14.03.2019 Mr. S. Maqbool Hussain Shah, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. S. Maqbool Hssain Shah, advocate has filed Vakalatnama on behalf of the applicant and submits that he has superseded the previous counsel, which is taken on record. However, he requests for short adjournment to prepare the case. At his request, adjournment to a date to be fixed in the second week of April, 2019. Office is directed to mention the name of the learned counsel on the file cover as well as the cause list.” Even today applicant is unrepresented without intimation or justification. It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings.

Office is directed to mention the name of the learned counsel on the file cover as well as the cause list.” Even today applicant is unrepresented without intimation or justification. It appears that the applicant is not interested in the present proceedings. Accordingly, in view of judgment of CIR vs. Rafeh Limited reported as PLD 2020 SC 518, the reference application is dismissed for non-prosecution. A copy of this decision may be sent under the seal of this Court and the signature of the Registrar to the learned Appellate Tribunal, as required per section 47 of the Sales Tax Act, 1990. Judge Judge Khuhro/PS
